title: After the Warden
description: The Warden's final words after defeat. Personal advice to each party member. Discovery of court magic corruption.

steps.text: The chamber was very quiet. The kind of quiet that only exists deep underground, where there is nothing for sound to bounce off of except stone.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: Thirty-eight. You are the first to get through.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: I don't know what that means for the other side, but — good. Carry something worth carrying.

steps.text: He set down his sword deliberately. Placed his shield beside it. The armor began to still in a way that was different from stillness.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: I don't know who put me here. I stopped asking. Maybe you should ask for both of us.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: Fighter. Your shield work is poor. You lead with your right side and don't correct for it. If you survive something worse than me, correct it.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: Mage. Stop documenting during the fight. You nearly took a blow that would have ended your journey. Your theory is correct. Trust it.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: Cleric. You held them together longer than anyone had a right to expect. That is rare. Guard your own life with the same care you spend on theirs.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: And you — Bard. When you sang to the wolf pack. That was not the action of a frightened musician. That was strategy.

lines.speaker: Warden
lines.text: Someone taught you well. Or no one taught you and you simply knew. I find I cannot tell which is true. Strange.

lines: The Bard said nothing, which was unusual enough to be notable.
Behind where the Warden had stood, scratched into the cave wall: traces of court magic. Formal enchantments.
Someone with authority had been feeding the cave's corruption deliberately.
